content.text:
From the image, I can read the rankings directly. Let me analyze each year's top 3:

**2015:** Lettuce(1), Sour cream(2), Tomato(3)
**2016:** Lettuce(1), Sour cream(2), Pico de gallo(3) — lines cross, Pico rises from 4
**2017:** Lettuce(1), Sour cream(2), Pico de gallo(3) — Tomato continues diving
**2018:** Lettuce(1), Sour cream(2), Pico de gallo(3) — Avocado also rising
**2019:** Lettuce(1), Sour cream(2), Tomato(3) — Tomato partially recovers

The headline confirms: "Pico and avocado rose in popularity but tomato took a dive."

Counting top-3 appearances:
- **Lettuce**: 5/5
- **Sour cream**: 5/5
- **Pico de gallo**: 3/5 (2016–2018)
- **Tomato**: 2/5 (2015, 2019)

<think>
Lettuce appeared in the top 3 all 5 years (always #1). Sour cream appeared in the top 3 all 5 years (always #2). Pico de gallo rose from rank 4 in 2015 to rank 3 in 2016–2018 (3 years). Tomato started at #3 in 2015, "took a dive" in the middle years, and appeared back at #3 in 2019 (2 years). So the items that appeared in the top 3 most are Lettuce, Sour cream, and Pico de gallo.
</think>

<answer>
Lettuce (5 times, always #1), Sour cream (5 times, always #2), and Pico de gallo (3 times, ranked #3 from 2016–2018) appeared in the top 3 most. Tomato only appeared in the top 3 twice (2015 and 2019), as it "took a dive" in the middle years.
</answer>
